refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Correlate with telemetry data (vehicle speed, acceleration, operator input latency, safety system activation events) to isolate performance bottlenecks.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services system shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (cycle start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-2.1: Allocate cycle time budget across vehicle sub-systems (load mechanism, propulsion, dump mechanism) and teleoperation latency; design vehicle dynamics and control algorithms to meet allocated budgets; implement safety constraint enforcement with bounded overhead.

DP-2.2: Implement closed-loop cycle time monitoring via telemetry; use real-time performance feedback to tune vehicle control parameters and identify bottlenecks; establish safety-aware cycle time optimization within safety envelope.

DP-2.3: Decouple vehicle mechanical performance (load/travel/dump timing) from operator control latency via predictive control and local autonomy; use teleoperation for high-level tasking rather than real-time command relay.

DP-2.4: Implement hierarchical cycle time management: vehicle-level sub-cycle timing guarantees, teleoperation-level command batching and latency compensation, safety-level constraint enforcement with pre-computed safe state transitions.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2.4: Implement hierarchical cycle time management with three layers: (1) vehicle-level sub-cycle timing guarantees (load, travel, dump mechanisms with deterministic control loops), (2) teleoperation-level command batching and latency compensation (operator tasking abstraction with local autonomy), (3) safety-level constraint enforcement with pre-computed safe state transitions that do not exceed safety_overhead_budget.

potential_sources: Vehicle propulsion and load mechanism timing are coupled; must be co-designed to avoid interference.
Teleoperation latency couples with vehicle control responsiveness; latency budget must be allocated and validated.
Safety constraint enforcement couples with cycle time; safety overhead must be bounded and measured.
Telemetry collection couples with real-time control; must not introduce timing jitter.
coupling_check.notes: DP-2.4 addresses coupling via explicit hierarchical separation of concerns: vehicle timing is decoupled from operator latency via local autonomy; safety constraints are enforced at a dedicated layer with pre-computed transitions. Coupling risks are manageable if interface contracts (latency budgets, safety overhead bounds, telemetry timing) are established and validated.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle sub-cycle timing can be made deterministic within ±5% of target via closed-loop control.
Assumption: Teleoperation latency can be bounded to ≤latency_budget via network QoS and local autonomy.
Assumption: Safety constraint enforcement overhead can be pre-computed and bounded to ≤safety_overhead_budget.
Risk: If vehicle dynamics are highly nonlinear or site conditions vary widely, deterministic timing may not be achievable; mitigation: adaptive control with real-time tuning.
Risk: If network latency is unpredictable or exceeds budget, operator control responsiveness degrades; mitigation: implement predictive control and fallback autonomy.

Risk: If safety constraints require frequent intervention (e.g., collision avoidance), overhead may exceed budget; mitigation: design safety system to minimize false positives and use efficient constraint enforcement.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-2.4 is sound but requires explicit definition of latency_budget, safety_overhead_budget, and sub-cycle timing tolerances. Recommend adding a DP refinement step to allocate these budgets across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety sub-systems before detailed design.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. At this level, the IM is responsible for: (1) cycle time budget allocation and tracking across sub-systems, (2) telemetry aggregation and KPI reporting, (3) coordination of vehicle, teleoperation, and safety timing constraints, (4) end-to-end cycle time verification and optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits cycle time telemetry (cycle start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions, p95 KPI) to Site Manager and System Engineer for performance monitoring and KPI verification.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives high-level tasking (load→travel→dump cycle commands) from Site Manager or Tele-operator via Teleoperation module; tasking is abstracted to reduce operator latency coupling.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (constraint violations, safety interventions, safe state transitions) to Safety Manager and Tele-operator via Safety Services module.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link for teleoperation command/feedback and telemetry transmission; network latency is a key coupling factor in cycle time performance.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level boundary for cycle time KPI management. Cycle time budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and end-to-end verification are system-level concerns that belong at this level. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry reporting and tasking coordination cross external boundaries.

Vehicle module owns sub-cycle timing (load, travel, dump mechanisms) but does not own teleoperation latency or safety constraint enforcement. Assigning cycle time KPI to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for latency and safety overhead, making verification difficult. Vehicle is a necessary but insufficient container.

Teleoperation module owns latency compensation and command batching but does not own vehicle sub-cycle timing or safety constraint enforcement. Assigning cycle time KPI to Teleoperation alone would diffuse responsibility for vehicle dynamics and safety overhead. Teleoperation is a necessary but insufficient container.

Safety Services module owns constraint enforcement and safe state transitions but does not own vehicle timing or teleoperation latency. Assigning cycle time KPI to Safety Services alone would diffuse responsibility for vehicle and teleoperation performance. Safety Services is a necessary but insufficient container.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Cycle time KPI is a system-level performance outcome that emerges from the interaction of vehicle dynamics, teleoperation latency, and safety constraints. The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate container because it: (1) owns the cycle time budget allocation across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, (2) aggregates telemetry from all sub-systems to compute p95 KPI, (3) coordinates timing constraints across sub-systems, (4) is responsible for end-to-end verification and optimization. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-system owners that contribute to cycle time performance but do not own the KPI itself.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution module is too abstract or lacks concrete ownership of budget allocation and telemetry aggregation, cycle time KPI may become orphaned. Mitigation: assign explicit ownership of cycle time budget tracking and telemetry pipeline to a named team/component within NGHE Solution.

Risk: If Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules do not have clear sub-cycle timing budgets and constraints, they may optimize locally without regard to system-level KPI. Mitigation: establish explicit interface contracts (latency budgets, safety overhead bounds, sub-cycle timing tolerances) between NGHE Solution and sub-modules.

Risk: If telemetry collection is not synchronized across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, cycle time measurement may be inaccurate. Mitigation: implement a centralized telemetry aggregation service within NGHE Solution with synchronized timestamps.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for cycle time KPI. Recommend adding a concrete sub-module or component within NGHE Solution (e.g., 'Cycle Time Manager' or 'Performance Monitor') to own budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and KPI verification. This will make ownership explicit and reduce diffusion risk.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-2.4 (hierarchical cycle time management) directly addresses the refined FR (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target with telemetry monitoring). The DP decomposes the problem into three manageable layers (vehicle timing, teleoperation latency, safety constraints) and specifies how each layer contributes to cycle time performance. The DP is implementable and verifiable via telemetry-based measurement and sub-cycle timing validation.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: Cycle time budget allocation is a system-level concern that must be owned by NGHE Solution, not delegated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services. Mitigation: establish explicit budget allocation interface contracts.

Diffusion risk: Telemetry aggregation and KPI computation must be centralized in NGHE Solution to avoid inconsistent measurements. Mitigation: implement a dedicated telemetry pipeline with synchronized timestamps.

Diffusion risk: Safety constraint enforcement overhead must be pre-computed and validated at the NGHE Solution level to ensure it does not exceed budget. Mitigation: establish safety overhead bounds as part of interface contracts with Safety Services.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for cycle time KPI because it owns the system-level budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and end-to-end verification. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-system owners that contribute to cycle time performance via explicit interface contracts. The hierarchical structure (DP-2.4) maps cleanly to the modular architecture (NGHE Solution → Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services).

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is verifiable via telemetry-based measurement of p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Telemetry interfaces (Telemetry out, Tasking in, SafetyAlert out, Network Link inout) provide the necessary data collection and reporting mechanisms. Verification is feasible if telemetry is synchronized and comprehensive.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution with clear sub-allocations to Vehicle (sub-cycle timing budgets), Teleoperation (latency budget), and Safety Services (safety overhead budget). Each sub-system has explicit timing constraints that can be verified independently and integrated to validate system-level KPI. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Establish explicit cycle time budget allocation across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services with measurable targets (e.g., Vehicle: ±5% of sub-cycle target, Teleoperation: ≤latency_budget, Safety Services: ≤safety_overhead_budget).

Define telemetry schema for cycle time KPI (cycle start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) and implement synchronized telemetry collection across all sub-systems.
Create a 'Cycle Time Manager' or 'Performance Monitor' component within NGHE Solution to own budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and KPI verification.
Establish interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services that specify timing constraints, telemetry requirements, and verification methods.

decision.justification: The refined FR (CR-NGHE-002) is decomposable into four sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) that address vehicle sub-cycle timing, teleoperation latency, safety constraint enforcement, and telemetry reporting. DP-2.4 (hierarchical cycle time management) is sound and maps cleanly to NGHE Solution as the IM boundary. However, the sub-FRs require further decomposition to establish concrete timing budgets, latency bounds, and safety overhead limits. Recommend proceeding to the next decomposition level to refine sub-FRs and allocate budgets across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
decision.justification_detail: FR-2.1 (vehicle sub-cycle timing) requires decomposition into load mechanism timing, travel timing, and dump mechanism timing with explicit budget allocations. FR-2.2 (teleoperation latency) requires decomposition into network latency, command processing latency, and feedback latency with explicit bounds. FR-2.3 (safety constraint enforcement) requires decomposition into constraint detection, safe state transition, and overhead measurement. FR-2.4 (telemetry reporting) requires decomposition into data collection, aggregation, and KPI computation. Each sub-FR will map to a specific DP and IM assignment at the next level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets (load_time_budget, travel_time_budget, dump_time_budget).
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget via command batching, predictive control, and local autonomy.
FR-2.3: The safety services system shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget via pre-computed safe state transitions and efficient constraint detection.

FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect, aggregate, and report cycle time telemetry (cycle start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) with synchronized timestamps for KPI verification.

assumptions: Assumption: Vehicle sub-cycle timing can be made deterministic within ±5% of target via closed-loop control.
Assumption: Teleoperation latency can be bounded to ≤latency_budget via network QoS and local autonomy.
Assumption: Safety constraint enforcement overhead can be pre-computed and bounded to ≤safety_overhead_budget.
Assumption: Telemetry collection and aggregation can be synchronized with ≤10ms jitter.
Assumption: Site conditions (terrain, load mass, ambient temperature) are within agreed duty cycle envelope and do not introduce unpredictable timing variations >10%.
Assumption: Operator skill level and tele-operation interface design do not introduce significant latency variability (>20% of latency_budget).
open_risks: Risk: Vehicle dynamics may be highly nonlinear or site conditions may vary widely, making deterministic timing difficult to achieve. Mitigation: implement adaptive control with real-time tuning and validate across representative site conditions.

Risk: Network latency may be unpredictable or exceed budget, degrading operator control responsiveness. Mitigation: implement predictive control and fallback autonomy; establish network QoS requirements.

Risk: Safety constraints may require frequent intervention (e.g., collision avoidance), causing overhead to exceed budget. Mitigation: design safety system to minimize false positives; use efficient constraint enforcement algorithms.

Risk: Telemetry collection may introduce timing jitter or overhead that affects cycle time performance. Mitigation: implement asynchronous telemetry collection with minimal impact on real-time control loops.

Risk: Cycle time KPI may be sensitive to operator behavior or site conditions, making it difficult to achieve consistent p95 performance. Mitigation: establish clear duty cycle definition and operator training requirements; monitor KPI variance and identify root causes.
evidence_needed: Evidence: Cycle time budget allocation across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services with measurable targets and justification.
Evidence: Vehicle sub-cycle timing validation (load, travel, dump mechanisms) under representative duty cycle conditions with ≥100 cycles.
Evidence: Teleoperation latency measurement and validation under representative network conditions with ≥100 command cycles.
Evidence: Safety constraint enforcement overhead measurement and validation with ≥100 constraint activation events.
Evidence: Telemetry schema definition and synchronized timestamp validation across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
Evidence: p95 cycle time measurement and KPI verification under agreed duty cycle conditions with ≥100 representative cycles.
Evidence: Root cause analysis of cycle time variance and identification of performance bottlenecks (vehicle dynamics, teleoperation latency, safety overhead, telemetry jitter).
